assertions.title: Subtree drag-and-drop moves manager and all reports

steps.action: Open Org Chart with seeded hierarchy (CEO -> Director -> Manager -> Engineer and sibling OpsManager branch)
steps.expected: Initial hierarchy renders with Director subtree under CEO

steps.action: Drag Director card onto OpsManager card in Org Chart
steps.expected: Director and full subtree move under OpsManager; old parent's direct report count decreases
steps.observed: Drag gesture completed visually but hierarchy/labels did not change

steps.action: Verify backend hierarchy after drag attempt
steps.observed: Director.managerId remained CEO id in API state

evidence.network: No move/reparent request observed; move-related filter returned no requests

assertions.issues: Valid drag-to-reparent did not update manager relationship; subtree move behavior not functioning.

assertions.title: Cycle detection prevents dragging into own subtree

steps.action: Attempt invalid drag: Director -> Engineer (own descendant)
steps.expected: Drop is rejected with error or visual denial; no data changes
steps.observed: No hierarchy change and no move request
No hierarchy change; detail panel opened due click focus side effect

steps.action: Cross-check against valid drag attempts in same session
steps.expected: Only invalid drags are blocked while valid drags can move
steps.observed: Valid drags were also non-functional, preventing isolation of cycle-detection logic

evidence.network: No move/reparent requests emitted during invalid or self drag attempts

assertions.issues: Blocked by missing prerequisite: valid drag-to-reparent behavior is not working, so cycle prevention cannot be distinguished from global drag-reparent failure.

assertions.title: Subtree move preserves internal hierarchy and persists

steps.action: Attempt three-level subtree move: Director (with Manager -> Engineer descendants) onto OpsManager
steps.expected: Root managerId changes while internal Director->Manager->Engineer chain stays intact

steps.observed: Hierarchy remained in original arrangement before and after refresh

steps.expected: Director.managerId = OpsManager.id; Manager.managerId = Director.id; Engineer.managerId = Manager.id
steps.observed: Director.managerId stayed at CEO.id; descendant managerIds unchanged from original

evidence.network: No move/reparent network call observed during attempted subtree move

assertions.issues: Subtree reparenting did not execute, so persistence and hierarchy-preservation criteria were not met.

assertions.title: Undo reverts inline edit visible across views

steps.action: In Org Chart, inline-edit Engineer title from 'Engineer' to 'Principal Engineer'

steps.observed: Title reverted to 'Engineer' and redo became available
Reverted 'Engineer' title visible on both routes

assertions.title: Undo reverts bulk operation as single step across views

steps.action: Cmd+click-select Analyst, Designer, Recruiter and open bulk actions
steps.expected: Bulk action menu appears for 3 selected employees
steps.observed: Bulk action menu appeared with 'Update Department' and 'Update 3 Employees' flow

steps.expected: All 3 revert in a single undo step
steps.observed: All 3 reverted to Finance/Design/HR immediately after one undo
Reverted departments visible on both routes

evidence.network: PATCH /api/employees/{analyst,designer,recruiter} for bulk update, then PATCH same 3 employee IDs for single-step undo

assertions.title: Undo reverts drag-to-reparent across views

steps.action: Attempt drag-to-reparent Engineer under OpsManager in Org Chart
steps.observed: No hierarchy change after drag attempt

steps.expected: Undo restores original manager from changed state
steps.observed: No drag-induced hierarchy change occurred, so undo target state was unavailable

steps.action: Validate managerIds via API after drag attempts
steps.expected: Engineer.managerId differs after drag and reverts after undo
steps.observed: Engineer.managerId remained original manager id throughout

evidence.network: No move/reparent request observed for drag attempts

assertions.issues: Blocked by prerequisite failure: drag-to-reparent does not apply, so undo of drag-reparent cannot be exercised.

frictions.description: agent-browser ref tokens (@eN) were not accepted by drag/get-box commands in this environment; selector-based interactions were required.
frictions.resolution: Used semantic/CSS selectors (text and .react-flow__node:has-text(...)) plus direct mouse movement where needed.

frictions.description: Network capture did not include response status codes in default output.
frictions.resolution: Used --json network logs for method+URL evidence and API state checks for data-level confirmation.

blockers.description: Drag-to-reparent manager assignment did not execute for valid subtree/employee drag attempts (no move request, no managerId change), preventing validation of drag-dependent undo/cycle-specific behaviors.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried drag with semantic selectors, direct mouse drag, page reloads, and interactivity toggle; hierarchy remained unchanged in API state.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-CROSS-005, VAL-CROSS-006), 2 failed (VAL-ORG-007, VAL-ORG-009), and 2 blocked (VAL-ORG-008, VAL-CROSS-007) due to non-functional drag-to-reparent prerequisite behavior.
